skill.name: py-alembic-patterns
skill.description: Alembic migration patterns for PostgreSQL. Use when creating migrations, reviewing autogenerated migrations, or handling schema changes safely.
skill.author: CJHarmath
skill.summary: Guides safe PostgreSQL schema migrations with Alembic patterns, review checks, and production safeguards.

security_audit.summary: Static command execution findings are mostly false positives caused by Markdown code fences and inline code examples. The skill is legitimate migration guidance, but it includes database migration and backup commands that can alter production data if used without review.

medium_findings.title: Operationally Sensitive Database Commands
medium_findings.description: The skill provides Alembic upgrade, downgrade, merge, and PostgreSQL backup commands. These are appropriate for the topic, but they can modify schemas or production data if run without review and backups.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit database migration and backup examples, so the operational risk is clear. The surrounding content frames them as safety guidance, so there is no evidence of malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Static Backtick Execution Findings Are Markdown Examples
low_findings.description: The static analyzer reported Ruby or shell backtick execution, but the cited locations are Markdown code fences or inline code examples. They are documentation snippets, not executable Ruby code in the skill package.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are visible Markdown delimiters or inline code markers in SKILL.md. No Ruby code, shell interpolation, or executable package script is present.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Finding Is Unsupported
low_findings.description: The cited description line does not contain a cryptographic algorithm or cryptographic operation. No evidence found that the skill recommends weak hashing or encryption.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is the YAML description for Alembic migration guidance. It contains no cryptographic API, hash function, key handling, or cipher reference.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Finding Is Unsupported
low_findings.description: The cited line creates a PostgreSQL index concurrently. It does not query host details, enumerate users, inspect networks, or collect system information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding section is about reducing table locks during migration. The cited SQL statement targets a database index and has no reconnaissance behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: Database Schema Mutation Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes commands that can upgrade, downgrade, or merge Alembic migrations. They are valid examples, but agents should confirm environment, backups, and review status before running them.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The examples are direct Alembic and PostgreSQL command lines. They are legitimate, but their effect on database state makes them operationally sensitive.

content.value_statement: Alembic autogenerate can create migrations that lose data or lock tables. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code practical PostgreSQL migration patterns for safer reviews and releases.
actual_capabilities: Explains common Alembic commands for creating, applying, rolling back, and inspecting migrations.
Shows how to review autogenerated migrations for column rename and table rename data loss.
Provides patterns for non-null columns, data backfills, large tables, enum changes, and multiple heads.
Gives a migration review checklist focused on downgrades, constraints, indexes, locks, and testing.
Describes production safeguards such as backups, SQL previews, and statement timeouts.
limitations: It provides guidance and examples, not automated verification of a live database.
It focuses on PostgreSQL and may not apply to other database engines.
It cannot guarantee zero downtime for migrations without project-specific data volume and traffic details.
It does not replace human review before production schema changes.

use_cases.title: Review an Autogenerated Migration
use_cases.description: Check an Alembic migration for unsafe drop and add operations, missing downgrades, unnamed constraints, and data loss risks.

use_cases.title: Plan a Production Schema Change
use_cases.description: Design a safer migration sequence for non-null columns, data backfills, indexes, and PostgreSQL enum changes.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Release Checklist
use_cases.description: Create a short migration checklist that covers backups, SQL previews, downgrade tests, lock risks, and multiple heads.

prompt_templates.title: Check a New Migration
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of one generated migration
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Alembic migration for PostgreSQL safety. Identify data loss risks, missing downgrade steps, unnamed constraints, and lock concerns.

prompt_templates.title: Fix an Autogenerate Result
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate rewrite of unsafe generated operations
prompt_templates.prompt: This Alembic autogenerate output looks unsafe. Rewrite the migration plan so column renames, non-null columns, and backfills avoid data loss.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Large Table Change
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced production planning for high-volume tables
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a PostgreSQL Alembic migration sequence for a large table. Include lock avoidance, batching assumptions, rollback limits, and test steps.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Migration Set
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this set of Alembic migrations before production release. Check upgrade order, downgrade behavior, multiple heads, enum changes, indexes, and backup requirements.

output_examples.input: Review a migration that renames users.name to users.full_name.
output_examples.output: The review flags the autogenerated drop and add pattern as a data loss risk. It recommends a column rename operation and a matching downgrade.

output_examples.input: Plan adding a required role column to users with existing rows.
output_examples.output: The plan adds the column as nullable, backfills existing rows, verifies data, and then applies the not-null constraint in a later step.

output_examples.input: Prepare a production checklist for applying pending migrations.
output_examples.output: The checklist covers backup creation, SQL preview, current revision check, downgrade test, lock risk review, and final upgrade approval.

best_practices: Always review autogenerated migrations before applying them to any shared database.
Test upgrade, downgrade, and upgrade again against realistic data before release.
Create backups and preview SQL before production migration commands are run.
anti_patterns: Do not accept drop and add operations for simple renames without manual review.
Do not add non-null columns to populated tables without a default or backfill plan.
Do not run production upgrades or downgrades before confirming the target database and backup status.

faq.question: What database does this skill focus on?
faq.answer: It focuses on PostgreSQL migrations managed with Alembic and SQLAlchemy.

faq.question: Can it write a complete migration for me?
faq.answer: It can guide migration structure and review patterns, but the final migration must match your models and data.

faq.question: Does it make Alembic autogenerate safe by itself?
faq.answer: No. It explains why autogenerated migrations still need manual review and testing.

faq.question: Can I use it for production releases?
faq.answer: Yes, as review guidance. Production commands still require backups, approval, and environment checks.

faq.question: Does it cover PostgreSQL enum changes?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains adding enum values, creating enum types, and downgrade limits.

faq.question: Does it detect live database state?
faq.answer: No. It does not inspect a live database unless the user separately provides commands or output.
